seoTitle: How to View the Check Register in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: See exactly how to open and read your bank account's check register in QuickBooks Online, including cleared and reconciled status markers.
intro: If you need to verify a cleared check, track outstanding deposits, or just review your recent cash activity, QuickBooks Online's check register gives you a full transaction history for any bank account. This guide shows you where to find it and how to make sense of what you see. It's especially useful when you need to double-check something before closing the books for the month.

steps.heading: Open the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: From the left navigation sidebar, click Transactions, then select Chart of Accounts to see all your bank and asset accounts listed.

steps.heading: Open the register for your account
steps.body: Find your checking account row and click View Register on the far right to open its full transaction history.

steps.heading: Filter by date, type, or amount
steps.body: Use the filter funnel above the register grid to narrow results to a specific date range, payment type, or text search.

steps.heading: Understand the status column
steps.body: Look for blank, C (cleared), or R (reconciled) markers next to each row to understand where a transaction stands with your bank.

steps.heading: Review or edit a specific entry
steps.body: Click on any row to expand it and review the payee, amount, and category, or edit the transaction if something looks off.

faq.question: What does the 'C' status mean in the register?
faq.answer: A 'C' marks a transaction as cleared, meaning it has matched against your downloaded bank feed but hasn't yet been included in a formal reconciliation.

faq.question: Can I edit a transaction directly from the register?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking a row expands it in place and gives you an Edit option to open the full transaction form.

faq.question: Why can't I find a specific check in the register?
faq.answer: Try widening your date filter or check whether the transaction was entered under a different account, since checks are tied to the specific bank account they were drawn from.

faq.question: Is the register the same as a bank statement?
faq.answer: Not exactly — the register reflects what's recorded in QuickBooks, which should match your bank statement once everything is reconciled.
